title: Esme and the Magic Library
synopsis: When Esme discovers that her school library comes alive at night with fairy tale characters, she must help them solve a magical problem before morning comes.

pages.text: Esme loved her new school. She had curly brown hair and bright green eyes that sparkled when she smiled. Her favorite place was the library with Ms. Chen, the kind librarian. "I forgot my lunch box," Esme told her mom after school. "Can we go back and get it?"

illustration.description: A cheerful girl with curly brown hair and bright green eyes standing outside a colorful elementary school building with her mom, a tall woman with dark skin wearing a blue coat. The school has red brick walls and big windows with the afternoon sun casting warm golden light.
illustration.alt: Esme and her mom standing outside their school

pages.text: The school was very quiet at night. Esme's footsteps echoed in the empty hallways. "This is a bit spooky," her mom whispered. They walked past classrooms filled with tiny chairs and colorful posters. The library door was open just a crack.

illustration.description: A dimly lit school hallway with soft blue moonlight streaming through windows. Esme and her mom walking hand in hand past classroom doors, with educational posters on the walls. Long shadows stretch across the polished floor creating a mysterious but not scary atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Esme and her mom walking through the quiet school hallway at night

pages.text: "Wait!" Esme said. "Do you hear that?" From inside the library came a strange sound. It sounded like... giggling? And tiny footsteps? "Libraries don't make noise at night," Mom said. But Esme was curious. She peeked through the crack in the door.

illustration.description: Esme with wide, curious green eyes peering through a slightly open library door. Her curly hair frames her face as she listens intently. Golden magical light spills out from the crack in the door, and small sparkles float in the air suggesting something magical is happening inside.
illustration.alt: Esme peeking through the library door with golden light streaming out

pages.text: Esme gasped! The library was full of fairy tale characters! Goldilocks sat reading in the big chair. The Three Little Pigs built a fort out of books. Jack was climbing a beanstalk made of green ribbons! "This is impossible," Mom whispered.

illustration.description: A magical library scene with fairy tale characters everywhere. Goldilocks with blonde curls sits in a large purple chair, three adorable pigs in overalls stack books, and Jack climbs green ribbon vines. Books float in the air with sparkles, and warm golden light fills the cozy library with tall bookshelves.
illustration.alt: The library filled with fairy tale characters and floating books

pages.text: A tiny voice called out, "Help us, please!" It was Thumbelina, no bigger than Esme's thumb. She sat on an open book, waving her arms. "We can't get back into our stories!" she cried. "The magic is mixed up!"

illustration.description: Tiny Thumbelina with flowing brown hair in a pink dress, standing on an open picture book and waving her arms. She has big expressive eyes and looks worried. The book she stands on glows with soft magic, and Esme leans down to listen, her face full of concern and wonder.
illustration.alt: Thumbelina standing on an open book calling for help

pages.text: "What happened?" Esme asked. She wasn't scared. She was excited! Red Riding Hood walked over. She had dark skin and wore a bright red hoodie. "Someone mixed up all our story endings," she said. "Now we don't know how to get home!"

illustration.description: Red Riding Hood with dark skin and natural hair, wearing a bright red hoodie and carrying a woven basket. She looks frustrated but friendly, standing next to bookshelves with other confused fairy tale characters in the background. The scene has warm, inviting colors with magical sparkles floating around.
illustration.alt: Red Riding Hood in a red hoodie explaining the problem to Esme

pages.text: "Look at this mess!" said Cinderella. She had beautiful black hair and wore a blue dress. She pointed at the books. "My glass slipper story got mixed with Hansel and Gretel! Now there's a gingerbread shoe!" Everyone started laughing, even Mom.

illustration.description: Cinderella with long black hair in a flowing blue ball gown, pointing at an open book showing a gingerbread house with a glass slipper door. She's laughing along with other fairy tale characters around her. The magical library glows with warm amber light and floating sparkles.
illustration.alt: Cinderella pointing at a book with mixed-up story elements

pages.text: The Seven Dwarfs were there too. They came from all over the world and spoke different languages. "¡Hola!" said one. "Bonjour!" said another. "Our story got mixed with Rapunzel's. Now Snow White has really, REALLY long hair!" They all giggled.

illustration.description: Seven diverse dwarfs in colorful mining outfits and pointed hats, each with different skin tones and features representing various cultures. They're gesturing animatedly while speaking different languages. Behind them is an open book showing Snow White with extremely long braided hair that winds around the page.
illustration.alt: The diverse Seven Dwarfs explaining their mixed-up story

pages.text: "This is silly but serious," said the Big Bad Wolf. He wore glasses and looked very smart. "If we don't fix our stories by morning, we'll be stuck here forever!" Esme thought hard. She was good at solving puzzles at school.

illustration.description: A friendly Big Bad Wolf wearing round glasses and a green vest, looking scholarly rather than scary. He has gray fur and kind yellow eyes. Esme stands nearby with her hand on her chin in a thinking pose, while other fairy tale characters gather around looking worried but hopeful.
illustration.alt: The scholarly Big Bad Wolf and thinking Esme discussing the problem

pages.text: "I have an idea!" Esme said. "At school, we sort things by groups. Maybe we need to sort your story pieces!" She looked around the magical library. "Where are all your endings?" The characters pointed to a big pile of mixed-up book pages in the corner.

illustration.description: Esme with her arms raised excitedly, surrounded by eager fairy tale characters. In the corner is a large, messy pile of colorful book pages with words and pictures scattered about. Golden magical sparkles swirl around the pile, and the library shelves tower above them filled with glowing books.
illustration.alt: Esme having an idea while characters point to a pile of mixed-up story pages

pages.text: Everyone got to work! Mom helped too. They sorted happy endings, sad endings, and silly endings. "This 'happily ever after' goes with Cinderella," Mom said. "And this 'huff and puff' belongs to the Three Little Pigs!" It was like a giant puzzle.

illustration.description: Everyone working together sorting colorful story pages spread across the library floor. Mom sits cross-legged helping sort papers, while fairy tale characters organize piles of endings. The scene is busy but organized, with warm library lighting and everyone smiling as they work cooperatively.
illustration.alt: Everyone working together to sort the mixed-up story endings

pages.text: "We did it!" shouted Jack. As soon as each story got its right ending back, the characters began to glow. One by one, they waved goodbye and disappeared back into their books. "Thank you, Esme!" they called. "You saved our stories!"

illustration.description: Fairy tale characters glowing with golden light as they wave goodbye, some already becoming translucent as they return to their books. Esme waves back with a big smile, and open books on shelves glow warmly. The magical library sparkles with success and friendship.
illustration.alt: Glowing fairy tale characters waving goodbye as they return to their books

pages.text: Soon the library was quiet again. All the books were back on their shelves. "Did that really happen?" Esme asked. Then she saw it - her lunch box sat on Ms. Chen's desk with a tiny note that said 'Thank you!' in sparkly letters.

illustration.description: The peaceful library restored to normal with books neatly arranged on shelves. Esme's red lunch box sits on Ms. Chen's wooden desk with a small glittery note beside it. Soft moonlight streams through windows, and a few barely visible sparkles float in the air as a hint of the recent magic.
illustration.alt: The quiet library with Esme's lunch box and a sparkly thank you note

pages.text: The next day at school, Esme visited the library. Ms. Chen smiled and said, "I heard you helped sort some mixed-up stories last night." She winked. "The books seem much happier today." Esme grinned. She loved her magical school library even more now!

illustration.description: Ms. Chen, an Asian woman with short black hair and a warm smile, wearing a colorful cardigan in the bright daytime library. Esme stands beside her grinning widely. Sunlight streams through windows onto organized bookshelves, and the books seem to have a subtle, happy glow.
illustration.alt: Esme and Ms. Chen smiling in the bright daytime library

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Why did Esme go back to school at night?
choices: To get her forgotten lunch box
To read books with Ms. Chen
questions.explanation: Esme forgot her lunch box at school and went back with her mom to get it.

questions.question: What was wrong with the fairy tale characters?
choices: Their story endings got mixed up
questions.explanation: The fairy tale characters couldn't get back to their stories because their endings got mixed up.

questions.question: How did Esme solve the problem?
choices: By sorting the story pieces into groups
questions.explanation: Esme used her school skills to sort the mixed-up story endings back to their correct stories.

questions.question: Who helped Esme sort the stories?
choices: Her mom and the fairy tale characters
questions.explanation: Both Esme's mom and all the fairy tale characters worked together to sort the story endings.

questions.question: How did Esme know the magic really happened?
choices: She found a sparkly thank you note
questions.explanation: Esme found her lunch box with a tiny note that said 'Thank you!' in sparkly letters.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words to complete Esme's adventure!
pages.text: Esme heard strange _____ coming from the library. When she looked inside, she saw fairy tale _____ everywhere! They needed help because their story _____ were all mixed up. Esme was good at solving _____, so she helped them sort everything out. Everyone worked _____ to fix the stories!

pages.title: Fairy Tale Mix-Up
pages.instructions: Help fix these mixed-up fairy tale endings!
pages.text: Red Riding Hood should meet her _____ at the end of her story. Cinderella needs to find her glass _____ to have a happy ending. The Three Little Pigs built their house out of _____ to stay safe. Jack climbed the _____ to reach the giant's castle.
